heading: Definitions
label: 3002 Definitions

children.label: A
children.text: a program providing residential, supportive, or employment-related services, to individuals with disabilities;

children.label: B
children.text: a program carried out by a center for independent living, such as a center described in part C of title VII of the Rehabilitation Act of 1973 ( et seq.);29 U.S.C. 796f

children.label: C
children.text: a program carried out by an employment support agency connected to adult vocational rehabilitation, such as a one-stop partner, as defined in ; andsection 3102 of this title

children.label: D
children.text: a program carried out by another organization or vender licensed or registered by the designated State agency, as defined in section 7 of the Rehabilitation Act of 1973 ().29 U.S.C. 705

children.heading: American Indian consortium
children.label: 2 American Indian consortium
children.text: The term “American Indian consortium” means an entity that is an American Indian Consortium (as defined in section 102 of the Developmental Disabilities Assistance and Bill of Rights Act of 2000 ()), and that is established to provide protection and advocacy services for purposes of receiving funding under subtitle C of title I of such Act ( et seq.).42 U.S.C. 1500242 U.S.C. 15041

children.heading: Assistive technology
children.label: 3 Assistive technology
children.text: The term “assistive technology” means technology designed to be utilized in an assistive technology device or assistive technology service.

children.heading: Assistive technology device
children.label: 4 Assistive technology device
children.text: The term “assistive technology device” means any item, piece of equipment, or product system, whether acquired commercially, modified, or customized, that is used to increase, maintain, or improve functional capabilities of individuals with disabilities.

children.label: A
children.text: the evaluation of the assistive technology needs of an individual with a disability, including a functional evaluation of the impact of the provision of appropriate assistive technology devices and services to the individual in the customary environment of the individual;

children.label: B
children.text: a service consisting of purchasing, leasing, or otherwise providing for the acquisition of assistive technology devices by individuals with disabilities;

children.label: C
children.text: a service consisting of selecting, designing, fitting, customizing, adapting, applying, maintaining, repairing, replacing, or donating assistive technology devices;

children.label: D
children.text: coordination and use of necessary therapies, interventions, or services with assistive technology devices, such as therapies, interventions, or services associated with education and rehabilitation plans and programs;

children.label: E
children.text: instruction or technical assistance for an individual with a disability or, where appropriate, the family members, guardians, advocates, or authorized representatives of such an individual;

children.label: F
children.text: instruction or technical assistance for professionals (including individuals providing education and rehabilitation services and entities that manufacture or sell assistive technology devices), employers, providers of employment and training services, or other individuals who provide services to, employ, or are otherwise substantially involved in the major life functions of individuals with disabilities; and

children.label: G
children.text: a service consisting of expanding the availability of access to technology, including electronic and information technology, to individuals with disabilities.

children.label: A
children.text: result in laws, regulations, policies, practices, procedures, or organizational structures that promote consumer-responsive programs or entities; and

children.label: B
children.text: facilitate and increase access to, provision of, and funding for assistive technology devices and assistive technology services, in order to empower individuals with disabilities to achieve greater independence, productivity, and integration and inclusion within the community and the workforce.

children.heading: Comprehensive statewide program of technology-related assistance
children.label: 7 Comprehensive statewide program of technology-related assistance
A
children.text: is implemented by a State;

children.label: B
children.text: is equally available to all individuals with disabilities residing in the State, regardless of their type of disability, age, income level, or location of residence in the State, or the type of assistive technology device or assistive technology service required; and

children.label: C
children.text: incorporates all the activities described in (unless excluded pursuant to ).section 3003(e) of this titlesection 3003(e)(5) of this title

children.label: i
children.text: respect for individual dignity, personal responsibility, self-determination, and pursuit of meaningful careers, based on informed choice, of individuals with disabilities;

children.label: ii
children.text: respect for the privacy, rights, and equal access (including the use of accessible formats) of such individuals;

children.label: iii
children.text: inclusion, integration, and full participation of such individuals in society;

children.label: iv
children.text: support for the involvement in decisions of a family member, a guardian, an advocate, or an authorized representative, if an individual with a disability requests, desires, or needs such involvement; and

children.label: v
children.text: support for individual and systems advocacy and community involvement; and

children.label: i
children.text: is easily accessible to, and usable by, individuals with disabilities and, when appropriate, their family members, guardians, advocates, or authorized representatives;

children.label: ii
children.text: responds to the needs of individuals with disabilities in a timely and appropriate manner; and

children.label: I
children.text: decisions relating to the provision of assistive technology devices and assistive technology services to such individuals; and

children.label: II
children.text: decisions related to the maintenance, improvement, and evaluation of the comprehensive statewide program of technology-related assistance, including decisions that affect capacity building and advocacy activities.

children.heading: Disability
children.label: 9 Disability
children.text: The term “disability” has the meaning given the term under .section 12102 of title 42

children.label: A
children.text: who has a disability; and

children.label: B
children.text: who is or would be enabled by an assistive technology device or an assistive technology service to minimize deterioration in functioning, to maintain a level of functioning, or to achieve a greater level of functioning in any major life activity.

children.heading: Institution of higher education
children.label: 11 Institution of higher education
children.text: The term “institution of higher education” has the meaning given such term in , and includes a community college receiving funding under the Tribally Controlled Colleges and Universities Assistance Act of 1978 ( et seq.).section 1001(a) of title 2025 U.S.C. 1801

children.label: A
children.text: are described in subtitle C of title I of the Developmental Disabilities Assistance and Bill of Rights Act of 2000 ( et seq.), the Protection and Advocacy for Individuals with Mental Illness Act ( et seq.), or section 509 of the Rehabilitation Act of 1973 (); and42 U.S.C. 1504142 U.S.C. 1080129 U.S.C. 794e

children.label: B
children.text: assist individuals with disabilities with respect to assistive technology devices and assistive technology services.

children.heading: Secretary
children.label: 13 Secretary
children.text: The term “Secretary” means the Secretary of Health and Human Services, acting through the Administrator of the Administration for Community Living.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Except as provided in subparagraph (B), the term “State” means each of the 50 States of the United States, the District of Columbia, the Commonwealth of Puerto Rico, the United States Virgin Islands, Guam, American Samoa, and the Commonwealth of the Northern Mariana Islands.

children.heading: Outlying area
children.label: i Outlying area
children.text: The term “outlying area” means the United States Virgin Islands, Guam, American Samoa, and the Commonwealth of the Northern Mariana Islands.

children.heading: State
children.label: ii State
children.text: The term “State” does not include the United States Virgin Islands, Guam, American Samoa, and the Commonwealth of the Northern Mariana Islands.

children.heading: State assistive technology program
children.label: 15 State assistive technology program
children.text: The term “State assistive technology program” means a program authorized under .section 3003 of this title

children.label: A
children.text: individuals with disabilities and their family members, guardians, advocates, and authorized representatives;

children.label: B
children.text: underrepresented populations;

children.label: C
children.text: individuals who work for public or private entities (including centers for independent living described in part C of title VII of the Rehabilitation Act of 1973 ( et seq.), insurers, or managed care providers) that have contact with, or provide services to, individuals with disabilities;29 U.S.C. 796f

children.label: D
children.text: educators and related services personnel, including personnel in elementary, secondary, and postsecondary schools, and in vocational and early intervention programs;

children.label: E
children.text: technology experts (including web designers and procurement officials);

children.label: F
children.text: health, allied health, and rehabilitation professionals, and employees of hospitals, skilled nursing, intermediate care, and assisted living facilities (including discharge planners);

children.label: G
children.text: employers, especially small business employers, and providers of employment and training services;

children.label: H
children.text: entities that manufacture or sell assistive technology devices;

children.label: I
children.text: entities that carry out community programs designed to develop essential community services in rural and urban areas; and

children.label: J
children.text: other appropriate individuals and entities, including public and private entities involved in housing and transportation, as determined for a State by the State.

children.heading: Underrepresented population
children.label: 17 Underrepresented population
children.text: The term “underrepresented population” means a population that is typically underrepresented in service provision, and includes populations such as individuals who have low-incidence disabilities, racial and ethnic minorities, low income individuals, homeless individuals (including children and youth), children in foster care, individuals with limited English proficiency, individuals living in institutions seeking to transition to the community from institutional settings, youth with disabilities aging into adulthood, older individuals, or individuals living in rural areas.

children.heading: Universal design
children.label: 18 Universal design
children.text: The term “universal design” means a concept or philosophy for designing and delivering products and services that are usable by people with the widest possible range of functional capabilities, which include products and services that are directly accessible (without requiring assistive technologies) and products and services that are interoperable with assistive technologies.
